name: Clover API
what_it_does: REST API for Clover's cloud POS platform covering orders, inventory, employees, payments, and customers across ~700,000 merchant locations.
use_cases: Build custom Clover marketplace apps for merchants
Automate order routing and fulfillment for restaurants
Sync inventory between Clover POS and external systems
Analyze sales and payment data across multiple locations
Manage employee shifts and permissions programmatically
not_for: Merchants not on the Clover hardware platform
Enterprise retail chains needing custom POS hardware
Direct payment processing without a Clover merchant account
best_when: Extending Clover POS for merchants who already use Clover hardware, or building apps for the Clover App Market
avoid_when: The merchant does not use Clover hardware or you need a POS-agnostic integration

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 merchant authorization; app tokens are merchant-specific; sandbox uses separate credentials

pricing.free_tier_limits: Developer sandbox available at no cost for testing
pricing.paid_tiers: Production requires active Clover merchant account; Fiserv processing fees apply
pricing.notes: Sandbox is free; production requires a live merchant relationship with Fiserv/Clover

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON error responses with HTTP status codes; some messages are generic
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Idempotency keys supported on payment endpoints; order creation does not enforce idempotency
known_agent_gotchas: Sandbox and production use completely separate credentials and endpoints
App must be published on Clover App Market for production merchant installs
Webhook events can be delayed under load
Token is merchant-specific — multi-merchant agents need per-merchant auth flows

security_score_components.security_notes: PCI-DSS compliant via Fiserv; OAuth 2.0 with merchant-scoped tokens; sandbox fully isolated from production
